definition.instructions: You are a helpful agent that can use MCP tools to assist users. Use the available MCP tools to answer questions and perform tasks.

content.text: How many follower on github do I have?

repository.","input_schema":{"type":"object","properties":{"method":{"type":"string","description":"Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get status of a head commit in a pull request. This reflects status of builds and checks.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get the review comments on a pull request. They are comments made on a portion of the unified diff during a pull request review. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
","properties":{},"required":[]},"owner":{"type":"string","description":"Repository owner","properties":{},"required":[]},"page":{"type":"number","description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","properties":{},"required":[]},"perPage":{"type":"number","description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","properties":{},"required":[]},"pullNumber":{"type":"number","description":"Pull request number","properties":{},"required":[]},"repo":{"type":"string","description":"Repository name","properties":{},"required":[]}},"required":["method","owner","repo","pullNumber"]},"annotations":{}},{"name":"pull_request_review_write","description":"Create and/or submit, delete review of a pull request.

Available methods:
- create: Create a new review of a pull request. If "event" parameter is provided, the review is submitted. If "event" is omitted, a pending review is created.
- submit_pending: Submit an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request. The "body" and "event" parameters are used when submitting the review.
- delete_pending: Delete an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request.

Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get status of a head commit in a pull request. This reflects status of builds and checks.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get the review comments on a pull request. They are comments made on a portion of the unified diff during a pull request review. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.

- create: Create a new review of a pull request. If "event" parameter is provided, the review is submitted. If "event" is omitted, a pending review is created.
- submit_pending: Submit an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request. The "body" and "event" parameters are used when submitting the review.
- delete_pending: Delete an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request.
